State your clear answer choice using \boxed{C}
Chemical burns from sulfuric acid produce coagulative necrosis, leading to deep tissue destruction and characteristic ulcerations, whereas thermal burns typically show singeing of hair due to pyrolysis. The presence of singeing in a chemical burn would indicate a thermal component or misdiagnosis, making the claim of "absence" incorrect — thus, option C is factually wrong and the exception.
